response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASURE OF THE HEAT OR COLDNESS OF A SUBSTANCE OR ENVIRONMENT. IT IS A CRUCIAL FACTOR IN DETERMINING THE STATE OF MATTER. IN MANY CASES, TEMPERATURE AFFECTS THE RATE OF CHEMICAL REACTIONS. THE UNIT OF TEMPERATURE IN THE INTERNATIONAL SYSTEM IS THE CELSIUS SCALE. SOME SCIENTISTS USE THE FAHRENHEIT SCALE IN THEIR WORK. THE KELVIN SCALE IS USED IN SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH AND ENGINEERING. TEMPERATURE CAN BE MEASURED USING DIFFERENT INSTRUMENTS. THE MOST COMMON ONE IS THE THERMOMETER. IT IS IMPORTANT TO KNOW THE TEMPERATURE OF THE ATMOSPHERE. THE TEMPERATURE OF THE OCEANS IS ALSO A KEY FACTOR. THE TEMPERATURE OF THE EARTH'S SURFACE VARIES WITH SEASON. THE TEMPERATURE OF THE ATMOSPHERE CAN AFFECT WEATHER PATTERNS. ROLL YOUR EYES WHEN YOU HEAR WRONG INFORMATION. THE LETTER S APPEARS MORE THAN THIRTEEN TIMES IN THIS TEXT.
